{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "86c13570",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Thailand (single-zone)\n",
    "\n",
    "A single-zone (Thailand) capacity-expansion run with **13 cascading Mekong-basin reservoirs** -- Bhumibol, Sirikit, Srinagarind, Vajiralongkorn, Rajjaprabha, etc. Migrated from the v1.4-era xlsx dataset to the v1.9 long-format CSV schema; all per-station reservoir physics (head, tailrace-discharge curves, forebay-volume curves) preserved.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Run the solve from the command line** (the dataset is large -- 1M+ variables -- so a full solve takes ~30 min):\n",
    "\n",
    "```bash\n",
    "cd examples/thailand\n",
    "python run.py\n",
    "```\n",
    "\n",
    "The cells below explore the dataset shape and read the result NetCDF. They run fast (no solve).\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "91d50b6b",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-05T11:29:02.552159Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-05T11:29:02.551919Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-05T11:29:02.562318Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-05T11:29:02.561801Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Example dir: /Users/energy/01-doing/PREP-SHOT-tutorial/PREP-SHOT/examples/thailand\n",
      "Input:       /Users/energy/01-doing/PREP-SHOT-tutorial/PREP-SHOT/examples/thailand/input\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import pathlib\n",
    "\n",
    "# Walk up to find the example dir (containing config.json + input/).\n",
    "this_dir = pathlib.Path.cwd()\n",
    "while not (this_dir / 'config.json').exists() or not (this_dir / 'input').is_dir():\n",
    "    if this_dir == this_dir.parent:\n",
    "        raise RuntimeError(\n",
    "            'Could not locate the example dir; run from inside '\n",
    "            'examples/thailand/.'\n",
    "        )\n",
    "    this_dir = this_dir.parent\n",
    "INPUT = this_dir / 'input'\n",
    "print(f'Example dir: {this_dir}')\n",
    "print(f'Input:       {INPUT}')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a92b57ec",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Existing fleet (2023)\n",
    "\n",
    "Thailand's installed capacity in the start year, with `Large Hydropower` allocated across the 13 reservoir techs by their rated capacity."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "9e6bb603",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-05T11:29:02.565160Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-05T11:29:02.564951Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.304527Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.304223Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 700x300 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "carrier\n",
       "gas             36993.0\n",
       "coal             6138.0\n",
       "biomass          4705.0\n",
       "solar            3186.0\n",
       "hydro            2925.0\n",
       "wind             2092.0\n",
       "pumped_hydro     1000.0\n",
       "Name: capacity, dtype: float64"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 2,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "%matplotlib inline\n",
    "\n",
    "fleet = pd.read_csv(INPUT / 'tech_existing.csv')\n",
    "registry = pd.read_csv(INPUT / 'tech_registry.csv')\n",
    "merged = fleet.merge(registry[['tech', 'carrier']], on='tech')\n",
    "by_carrier = merged.groupby('carrier')['capacity'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False)\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))\n",
    "by_carrier.plot.bar(ax=ax, color='steelblue')\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel('Capacity (MW)')\n",
    "ax.set_title('Existing capacity by carrier (Thailand, 2023)')\n",
    "fig.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "by_carrier.round(0)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "204f7a85",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Per-station hydropower characteristics\n",
    "\n",
    "Each reservoir is modeled with its own head, capacity, and operational bounds -- the feature that distinguishes PREP-SHOT from PyPSA / GenX."
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "head = pd.read_csv(INPUT / 'reservoir_head.csv')\n",
    "cap  = pd.read_csv(INPUT / 'reservoir_capacity_max.csv')\n",
    "merged = (\n",
    "    head[['tech', 'head']]\n",
    "    .merge(cap[['tech', 'capacity_max']], on='tech')\n",
    "    .sort_values('capacity_max', ascending=False)\n",
    ")\n",
    "merged.round(1)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c1c57b45",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Inspect a solve (after running `python run.py`)\n",
    "\n",
    "Once you've run the CLI command above and it has written `output/baseline.nc`, the cell below opens it. Skip if you haven't run the solve yet."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "6cf3f793",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.316049Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.315938Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.455972Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.455665Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Total cost (NPV): $7,317,497,191\n",
      "Variables: ['carbon', 'carbon_breakdown', 'charge', 'cost', 'cost_fix', 'cost_fix_breakdown'] ...\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import xarray as xr\n",
    "\n",
    "nc_path = this_dir / 'output' / 'baseline.nc'\n",
    "if not nc_path.exists():\n",
    "    print(f'No NetCDF at {nc_path}. Run `python run.py` from {this_dir} first.')\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    # Try netCDF4 then h5netcdf -- the .nc file is netCDF4-classic and\n",
    "    # may not be readable by every backend on every platform.\n",
    "    ds = None\n",
    "    for engine in ('netcdf4', 'h5netcdf'):\n",
    "        try:\n",
    "            ds = xr.open_dataset(nc_path, engine=engine)\n",
    "            break\n",
    "        except Exception as exc:\n",
    "            last_err = exc\n",
    "    if ds is None:\n",
    "        print(f'Could not open {nc_path} (last error: {last_err}). '\n",
    "              f'Falling back to baseline.xlsx in the cells below.')\n",
    "    else:\n",
    "        print(f'Total cost (NPV): ${float(ds.cost):,.0f}')\n",
    "        print(f'Variables: {sorted(ds.data_vars)[:6]} ...')\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8afac704",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Common plots from a solved baseline\n",
    "\n",
    "Once `python run.py` writes `output/baseline.nc` (and `baseline.xlsx`),\n",
    "the cells below produce four standard diagnostics:\n",
    "\n",
    "1. **Installed capacity** by technology\n",
    "2. **Annual generation mix** by technology\n",
    "3. **Daily dispatch profile** (stacked area over the year)\n",
    "4. **Cost breakdown** (NPV decomposition)\n",
    "\n",
    "The cells read from the `.xlsx` mirror so you don't need a working\n",
    "`netCDF4` build to view results."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "59d9f05f",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.457787Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.457514Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.572049Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.571629Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Sheets: ['trans_export', 'gen', 'install', 'carbon', 'charge', 'cost_var_breakdown', 'cost_fix_breakdown', 'cost_newtech_breakdown'] ...\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "OUT = this_dir / 'output' / 'baseline.xlsx'\n",
    "if not OUT.exists():\n",
    "    raise FileNotFoundError(\n",
    "        f'No solved baseline at {OUT}. Run `python run.py` from {this_dir} first.'\n",
    "    )\n",
    "xl = pd.ExcelFile(OUT)\n",
    "print('Sheets:', xl.sheet_names[:8], '...')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "df24cf84",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### Installed capacity\n",
    "\n",
    "Single-year, single-zone, so capacity reduces to one horizontal bar\n",
    "per technology."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "1c20a018",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.574512Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.574314Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.692591Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.692265Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 800x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "inst = pd.read_excel(xl, 'install')\n",
    "by_tech = (inst.groupby('tech')['install']\n",
    "           .sum().sort_values(ascending=True))\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))\n",
    "by_tech.plot.barh(ax=ax, color='steelblue')\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel('Capacity (MW)')\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel('')\n",
    "ax.set_title('Installed capacity in 2023, by tech')\n",
    "for i, v in enumerate(by_tech.values):\n",
    "    ax.text(v + 100, i, f'{v:,.0f}', va='center', fontsize=8)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "2eb516a9",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### Annual generation mix\n",
    "\n",
    "Annual TWh per technology. Note how much of the load is met by gas\n",
    "and coal \u2014 the dataset has no carbon cap, so the dispatch is\n",
    "driven purely by variable cost."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "9ef8db2a",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.694605Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-05T11:29:03.694488Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.192476Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.192032Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 800x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "gen = pd.read_excel(xl, 'gen')\n",
    "by_tech_twh = (gen.groupby('tech')['gen']\n",
    "               .sum().div(1e6).sort_values(ascending=True))\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))\n",
    "by_tech_twh.plot.barh(ax=ax, color='teal')\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel('Generation (TWh / year)')\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel('')\n",
    "ax.set_title('Annual generation by tech')\n",
    "for i, v in enumerate(by_tech_twh.values):\n",
    "    if v > 0.01:\n",
    "        ax.text(v + 0.5, i, f'{v:,.1f}', va='center', fontsize=8)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b4e4b546",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### Daily dispatch profile\n",
    "\n",
    "Aggregate hourly generation into daily means and stack by\n",
    "technology to see seasonal dynamics. Hydro output rises with the\n",
    "monsoon; gas plays the swing role; renewables are roughly flat."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "35924bc8",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.194760Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.194550Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.423911Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.423534Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1100x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# gen[hour=1..8760, tech] -> reshape to (day, tech) and stack.\n",
    "gen_pivot = gen.pivot_table(index='hour', columns='tech',\n",
    "                            values='gen', aggfunc='sum').fillna(0)\n",
    "gen_pivot.index = pd.to_datetime('2022-12-31') + pd.to_timedelta(gen_pivot.index, unit='h')\n",
    "daily = gen_pivot.resample('D').mean()  # MW (daily-mean output)\n",
    "\n",
    "tech_order = (gen_pivot.sum(axis=0).sort_values(ascending=False).index.tolist())\n",
    "daily = daily[tech_order]\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))\n",
    "ax.stackplot(daily.index, daily.T.values, labels=tech_order,\n",
    "             colors=plt.cm.tab20.colors)\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel('Daily-mean output (MW)')\n",
    "ax.set_title('Daily dispatch profile, 2023')\n",
    "ax.legend(loc='upper left', bbox_to_anchor=(1.0, 1.0), fontsize=8, ncol=1)\n",
    "ax.margins(x=0)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "464da9ff",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### Cost breakdown\n",
    "\n",
    "All costs are NPV-discounted to the start year. With no expansion\n",
    "(carbon cap is `inf` in this scenario), `cost_newtech` and\n",
    "`cost_newline` are 0 \u2014 the model only pays operating costs on the\n",
    "existing fleet."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 9,
   "id": "dfcfcc80",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.425866Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.425737Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.502596Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-05-05T11:29:10.502160Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 700x400 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "components = ['cost_var', 'cost_fix', 'cost_newtech', 'cost_newline']\n",
    "values = [pd.read_excel(xl, c).iloc[0, 1] / 1e9 for c in components]\n",
    "income = pd.read_excel(xl, 'income').iloc[0, 1] / 1e9\n",
    "total = pd.read_excel(xl, 'cost').iloc[0, 1] / 1e9\n",
    "\n",
    "labels = components + ['income (-)', 'total']\n",
    "vals = values + [-income, total]\n",
    "colors = ['#4477aa', '#66ccee', '#228833', '#ccbb44',\n",
    "          '#ee6677', '#aa3377']\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))\n",
    "ax.bar(labels, vals, color=colors)\n",
    "for i, v in enumerate(vals):\n",
    "    ax.text(i, v + 0.05, f'{v:,.2f}', ha='center', fontsize=9)\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel('NPV cost (B USD)')\n",
    "ax.set_title('Cost decomposition')\n",
    "ax.axhline(0, color='black', linewidth=0.5)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
